_meta.title: Qalam audio manifest — the single source of truth for bundled pronunciation clips
_meta.purpose: One manifest drives (a) assets/audio/README.md's audioId table and (b) the _audioIdToAsset map in lib/services/asset_audio_player.dart. Regenerate both from here; `python -m audio_pipeline check` fails on any drift.

schema.audioId: Schema-v2 audio id the app resolves (snd.<letterId> / word.<id> / sentence.<id>).
schema.assetPath: Repo-relative bundled asset path (always under assets/audio/).
schema.description: Human description of what the clip says.
schema.usedBy: Where the clip is referenced today (informational; not enforced by check).
schema.status: real | placeholder | draft-tts. real = the owner's-mother's recording; draft-tts = interim AI voice; placeholder = silent/stand-in.

_meta.statusNote: All 39 current clips are AI-voice interim recordings (ElevenLabs, generated by tools/tts/generate_audio.py) — honestly 'draft-tts', not the real human recordings. The definition of done for the 28 letter sounds is REAL human recordings; until the owner + mother record them, these draft-tts clips ship. Never relabel a draft-tts clip 'real'.
_meta.voiceNote: Whose voice records the real clips is the owner + mother's decision. This pipeline is voice-agnostic: drop raw recordings into tools/audio_pipeline/staging/, run `normalize`, then flip status to 'real'.
